sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The board changed! Clicking on the white block at (50,30) changed it to green (N). I can see in the image that the top-left block of the 3x3 grid is now green instead of white. 
So the mechanic is: clicking on blocks in the 3x3 grid changes their color. The white block became green. Let me check what the pattern is - I need to understand what the goal is. 
Looking at the bottom-left container, it has purple dots on a gray background. Maybe the goal is to make the 3x3 grid match some pattern? Or maybe clicking toggles colors in a cycle?
Let me inspect the current state more carefully to see exactly what changed, then try clicking on another block to understand the color cycle.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Board changed! The white block at (50,30) turned green. Let me inspect what changed exactly.
# Check the current grid state
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("--- Bottom-right grid area after click ---")
for i in range(48, 62):
    print(f"  Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i]}")

# Also check segmentation to see what changed
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("\n--- Nodes in grid area ---")
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if min(rows) >= 48:
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
